authors: Devulder J, De Laat M, Van Bastelaere M, Rolly G
title: Spinal Cord Stimulation: A Valuable Treatment for Chronic Failed Back Surgery Patients
journal_name: Journal of Pain and Symptom Management
study_question: Is spinal cord stimulation effective and cost-effective for chronic failed back surgery syndrome?
population_assessed: 69 patients with chronic failed back surgery syndrome treated with spinal cord stimulation over 13 years.
outcome_measures: Pain relief, return to work, drug intake, cost per patient per year
inclusion_criteria: Chronic failed back surgery syndrome, no other surgical options, no psychological impairment, successful trial stimulation.
answer_to_study_question: 43 patients continued therapy with good pain relief. 11 returned to work. Cost $3660 per patient yearly. Battery systems required fewer electrode interventions than radiofrequency systems.
